title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Coffee is among the most widely consumed beverages in the world, (0) which has a cultural and economic history stretching back many centuries to its origins in the highlands of Ethiopia. Its global journey offers a compelling case study in the spread of commodities and ideas. (9) Although the precise origins of coffee consumption remain subject to some uncertainty, historical accounts suggest that the practice of roasting and brewing coffee beans began in Yemen in the fifteenth century. (10) Such was the stimulating effect of coffee that it quickly attracted both enthusiastic patrons and religious objections from authorities concerned about its social influence. Coffeehouses became important sites of intellectual exchange, and (11) Provided that local authorities permitted them to operate, they functioned as informal centres of political debate and commercial networking across the Ottoman Empire and later Europe. Not (12) only did the coffeehouse culture of seventeenth-century London contribute to the development of institutions such as Lloyd's of London, but it also helped to establish a culture of public debate among merchants and professionals. (13) Regardless of geographic or cultural context, coffeehouses historically served as spaces where social distinctions were temporarily suspended and ideas could circulate more freely. Not (14) until European colonial expansion in the eighteenth century did coffee cultivation spread to the Americas and South-East Asia, creating the global supply chains that persist to this day. (15) Within the contemporary speciality coffee industry, there is growing emphasis on direct trade relationships, environmental sustainability and the fair distribution of value along the supply chain. These changes reflect broader shifts in consumer values. (16) Whether coffee's story is told through the lens of cultural history or global economics, it exemplifies the complex networks through which goods, practices and ideas have moved across the world.

example.text: Coffee is among the most widely consumed beverages in the world, (0) which has a cultural and economic history stretching back many centuries to its origins in the highlands of Ethiopia.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Preposition used to locate something inside a defined context, area, field or domain.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
